page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor with an x86_64 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external storage solutions, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression rated as poor. However, the instance leverages its 32 dedicated physical cores to achieve average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations also perform within the average tier.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.sn2ne.8xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from high core density and dedicated CPU allocation without requiring cutting-edge single-thread speeds. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.

This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high single-thread speeds.
